sections.paragraph:
σχισμή, ῆς, ἡ (σχίζω) crack, fissure (Rhet. Gr. I 552, 4; LXX) in stones (cp. Is 2:19, 21; SibOr 3, 607) Hv 3, 2, 8; 3, 6, 3; Hs 9, 6, 4; 9, 8, 3f; 9, 23, 1–3; in sticks Hs 8, 1, 9f; 14; 8, 4, 6; 8, 5, 1; 4f; 8, 7, 1f; 4; 8, 10, 1; in a mountain (Jon 2:6) Hs 9, 1, 7.—DELG s.v. σχίζω.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 981
